title: Jarai-ka-Math
tagline: Jarai-ka-Math — spiritual town of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Jarai-ka-Math is a temple dedicated to Goddess Laxmi in Barua sagar near Jhansi in Uttar Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Jarai-ka-Math is a temple dedicated to Goddess Laxmi in Barua sagar near Jhansi in Uttar Pradesh, India.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Barua Sagar
topPlaces.description: Barua Sagar is a town and a municipal board in Jhansi district in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. It was named for the lake called Barua Sagar Tal.

topPlaces.name: Barua Sagar Tal
topPlaces.description: Barua Sagar Tal is a large lake in Barua Sagar near Jhansi in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Pravin Rai Mahal
topPlaces.description: Pravin Rai Mahal is a palace in Madhya Pradesh built by prince (and later King) Indrajit Singh in 1618. It was built for his love interest, Pravin Rai, who was a beautiful poet and musician, also known as the "Nightingale of Orchha".

topPlaces.name: Jahangir Mahal, Orchha
topPlaces.description: Jahangir Mahal, Citadel of Jahangir, Orchha Palace, Mahal-e-Jahangir Orchha, Jahangir Citadel is a citadel and garrison located in Orchha, Madhya Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Orchha
topPlaces.description: Orchha is a city near the city of Niwari in the Niwari district of Madhya Pradesh state, India. The city was established by a Bundela Rajput ruler Rudra Pratap Singh some time after 1501, as the seat of an eponymous former princely state including parts of central and north India, in the Bundelkhand region.

topPlaces.name: Orchha State
topPlaces.description: Orchha State (also known as Urchha, Ondchha and Tikamgarh) was a kingdom situated in the Bundelkhand region and later a princely state in British India. The state was ruled by Bundela clan of Rajputs.

topPlaces.name: Orchha Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Orchha Wildlife Sanctuary also known Orchha Nature Reserve is a wildlife sanctuary located in Niwari district of the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. It was established in 1994.

topPlaces.name: Ram Raja Temple
topPlaces.description: Ram Raja Temple is a Hindu temple in Orchha, Madhya Pradesh, India. In this temple Raja Ram is accompanied by Sita (on the left), brother Lakshmana (on the right), Maharaj Sugriva and Narsingh Bhagwan (on the right).

items.note: Barua Sagar is a town and a municipal board in Jhansi district in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. It was named for the lake called Barua Sagar Tal.
Barua Sagar Tal is a large lake in Barua Sagar near Jhansi in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

Pravin Rai Mahal is a palace in Madhya Pradesh built by prince (and later King) Indrajit Singh in 1618. It was built for his love interest, Pravin Rai, who was a beautiful poet and musician, also known as the "Nightingale of Orchha".
Jahangir Mahal, Citadel of Jahangir, Orchha Palace, Mahal-e-Jahangir Orchha, Jahangir Citadel is a citadel and garrison located in Orchha, Madhya Pradesh, India.

Orchha is a city near the city of Niwari in the Niwari district of Madhya Pradesh state, India. The city was established by a Bundela Rajput ruler Rudra Pratap Singh some time after 1501, as the seat of an eponymous former princely state including parts of central and north India, in the Bundelkhand region.

Orchha State (also known as Urchha, Ondchha and Tikamgarh) was a kingdom situated in the Bundelkhand region and later a princely state in British India. The state was ruled by Bundela clan of Rajputs.
Orchha Wildlife Sanctuary also known Orchha Nature Reserve is a wildlife sanctuary located in Niwari district of the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. It was established in 1994.

Ram Raja Temple is a Hindu temple in Orchha, Madhya Pradesh, India. In this temple Raja Ram is accompanied by Sita (on the left), brother Lakshmana (on the right), Maharaj Sugriva and Narsingh Bhagwan (on the right).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jarai-ka-Math?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jarai-ka-Math is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

The nearest airport is Gwalior Airport (111 km) and the nearest railway station is Jhansi Junction (15 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jarai-ka-Math?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jarai-ka-Math at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jarai-ka-Math?
faq.a: Stays in Jarai-ka-Math start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jarai-ka-Math?
faq.a: The top attractions are Barua Sagar, Barua Sagar Tal, Pravin Rai Mahal, Jahangir Mahal, Orchha, Orchha.
